articles.title: Apple is finally removing AI apps that can digitally undress anyone
articles.description: Apple has removed several AI nudify apps after legal action accused the company of allowing deepfake pornography tools to remain on the App Store.
articles.content:
Apple has removed several AI-powered “nudify” apps from the App Store after coming under legal pressure from San Francisco’s city attorney, but the episode raises a much bigger question than whether a handful of apps violated App Store rules.

articles.title: What Is a Microgrid? How They Work and Why They're Suddenly Everywhere
articles.description: How microgrids work, why they matter, and why AI data centers are racing to build their own power grids.
articles.content: When Hurricane Maria flattened Puerto Rico's power grid in 2017, one building in the mountain town of Adjuntas never went dark. Casa Pueblo, a local environmental group, had already wired its headquarters with solar panels and batteries. That system ... [11277 chars]

articles.title: ‘If someone needs a million drones, we must be able to deliver’: Weapons maker Saab’s CEO
articles.description: The Swedish defence contractor is pushing advances in AI for modern warfare while expanding partnerships globally. Read more at straitstimes.com.

articles.title: AI 'Actor' Sparks Debate and Transformation in Entertainment Industry
articles.description: The creation of AI 'actor' Tilly Norwood by Eline van der Velden has sparked a debate, with her character aiming to explore the possibilities of AI rather than replace human performers. Meanwhile, Marvel Comics relocates to California, and Rolling Stones achieve chart success with their 15th UK number one album.
articles.content:
The entertainment industry is abuzz with discussions surrounding Tilly Norwood, an AI 'actor' developed by Eline van der Velden. Designed to ignite conversation rather than replace human talent, Tilly symbolizes a new frontier in film.

articles.title: gen optical links can connect thousands of chips
articles.description: Chinese chip startup Biren has introduced a new optical supernode architecture designed to connect thousands of AI chips in a single cluster.
articles.content:
As AI models continue to grow in size and complexity, chipmakers are shifting their focus from individual processor performance to the ability to efficiently connect thousands of accelerators into a single computing system.

articles.title: Data centers have united Americans of both parties in a shared hatred
articles.description: Data centers have become a lightning rod for people’s frustrations over corporate power, public trust and the costs of the AI boom.
articles.content:
Kyle Schmidt ticked through what makes him irate about the massive Google data center slated for construction about a mile away from his home near Sand Springs, Oklahoma.

articles.title: New York Mets reject concerns over in-game AI use after Ottavino claims
articles.description: Mets interim manager Andy Green says the club remains fully compliant with MLB rules after former reliever Adam Ottavino alleged the team used AI for in-game strategy
articles.content:
New York Mets interim manager Andy Green said the club is fully compliant with Major League Baseball when asked about a report that singled out his organization for using artificial intelligence for in-game strategy decisions.

articles.title: Anti-AI data center protesters rally across the US
articles.description: Humans First, a conservative-led nonprofit, organized nationwide protests against AI data centers on Saturday.
articles.content:
America's anti-data center movement went national on Saturday.
